trail.val: FY26 "close to 30%" in USD (Q4 FY25)
FY26 raised to "above 30%" in USD; agentic shift another step-up in tokens
FY26 rev now guided "slightly above 40%" in USD — clears the most-watched pre-print bogey (buy-side ~40%); 2Q rev US$40.2bn (record)

forward.note: FY26 guide raised twice this year; 3Q26 rev guide US$44.6-45.8bn (mid $45.2bn).

trail.val: Fubon FY27 rev NT$7,057bn (+37%, Street-high), GM 68.8%
Fubon FY27 EPS NT$144.13 vs cons NT$127.84
Fubon raises FY26/FY27 EPS to NT$108.80 / NT$155.41 (from NT$103.90/144.13) vs cons NT$100.13/129.26 — market-high
trail.src: Fubon post-print 'Capex hike a strong indicator'

forward.note: Fubon EPS the Street-high; consensus dragging behind on the FY26 >40% guide + capex raise.

trail.val: N3 regular hikes ~10%, N2 ~5-6% into Jan-27; hot-run/super-hot-run premiums rising
2027 wafer-price hikes start at 10% incl. Apple (vs +1-2% Apple 2026); blended ASP ≥10%; GM to 68-70%
2027 wafer prices +≥10% across ALL customers incl. Apple; GM potentially high-60s to 70%; supply/demand gap 15-20%

forward.note: Pricing the key GM lever; 2027 supply-demand gap widens to 15-20%. Q3'26 GM guided 65-67% on N2/overseas dilution.

trail.val: FY26 to high end (~$56bn); next 3 years of capex "significantly higher" than the $101bn prior 3 years
40-45 EUV units already booked for 2027 (DRAM competition) → caps 2027 capex ~$70-75B vs others' $80-85B
FY26 capex RAISED to US$60-64bn (from ~$56bn) on demand + tool-price inflation; next 3 years 'even more significantly higher' (no numeric 27/28 guide); +$100bn Arizona → $265bn total

forward.note: FY26 hard raise to $60-64bn clears the ~$58bn buy-side bogey; GS models FY27 $78bn, JPM $78/85bn 27/28 — EUV-tool availability the binding cap.

trail.val: N2 first-year rev contribution ~6% in 2025; ~60k capacity exiting Q4-25
N2 ~40-45k WPM end-2025 → 65-70k end-2026 → 110-120k end-2027 (nearly doubles 2027)
N2 = 3% of 2Q26 wafer revenue (first contribution); Fubon marks N2 to 120k / N3 to 210-220k by end-2027; steep ramp = 3-4pt H2'26 GM drag

forward.note: N2 ramp dilutes FY26 GM 3-4pt in H2; net-new tooling (GAA + backside power).

trail.val: raised trajectory to 120k end-2026 / 140k end-2027
JPM raising CoWoS capacity to 115k/175k/220k WPM end-26/27/28; SoIC ~30-40k by end-2027
MS raised 2027 CoWoS to 200kwpm by year-end (from 170k) = ~60% Y/Y AI-XPU growth; Fubon 2028 220k + 20-30k CoPoS
trail.src: MS Asia AI Supply Chain / Fubon

forward.note: NVDA single-sources CoWoS-L ~910k 2027; CPU a fresh 2.5D leg (Vera 5.75M / Venice 6.75M units). Packaging 'so tight' TSMC welcomes Intel EMIB-T flexibility (2Q26 call).

trail.val: CAGR raised to mid-/high-50s % (2024-29)
CAGR toward higher-50s; AI "extremely robust"
C.C. Wei declined a new number but said the CAGR is 'stronger and stronger and stronger… stronger than what we said before' (watched 70-80% not formally given)

forward.note: Wafers the binding AI constraint into 2029-30; demand-vs-supply gap on N3-and-below 'really big'. Agentic AI adds a fresh CPU silicon leg.
